[2018-07-12 18:30] supergreyman (Greyman): The only thing draining economy can do is to bypass absorber which would require it to deal early game damage. Instead of forcing them to lose gold, you can give Exhaust 2 to your opponent's drones.
[2018-07-12 18:34] swapgo: Well there could be, like, a reverse Auric Impulse to stop your opponent from getting a specific timing
[2018-07-12 18:34] swapgo: Though I'm sure that's hell to balance
[2018-07-12 18:35] swapgo: EMP (3 :Gold: :Energy: , 10 Supply)
On Buy: Up to two opposing non-blocking, non-exhausted Drones get Exhaust 2
[2018-07-12 18:38] supergreyman (Greyman): I think you would need to make it exhaust only 1 Drone to sidestep SDR breaking.
[2018-07-12 18:39] supergreyman (Greyman): Or say "up to 2 Drones"
[2018-07-12 18:39] swapgo: Also added that to make sure it's clear that if you use it in multiples it hits multiples
[2018-07-12 18:40] swapgo: instead of just exiling 2 drones from the game
[2018-07-12 18:41] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, if your opponent has only 2 Drones, they'll get Exhaust 10 after 5 EMP's. 😄
[2018-07-12 18:41] supergreyman (Greyman): But it wouldn't really matter that much.
[2018-07-12 18:42] supergreyman (Greyman): The concept is really weird though.
[2018-07-12 18:44] swapgo: Unless we introduce Negative Money I don't see another implementation to remove gold from an opponent that isn't floating gold
[2018-07-12 18:50] supergreyman (Greyman): It makes me wonder if something like this would change your gold estimate.
[2018-07-12 18:51] swapgo: Well people shooting your drones with Deadeyes (or manually if they're wild) already sort of screws with your gold estimate
[2018-07-12 18:51] swapgo: EMP would just make it much more stressful because it's like "Oh THIS unit is in the set I can have 2 less Gold at any point"
[2018-07-12 18:53] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I can get on board with EMP. Maybe not 'on buy', but having to sacrifice it?
[2018-07-12 18:54] supergreyman (Greyman): Right, I didn't think of deadeye/Wild Drone case.
[2018-07-12 18:55] swapgo: Yeah that sounds fairer
[2018-07-12 18:56] swapgo: It also means that, if you let your opponent, they mass these and can timewalk you at will
[2018-07-12 18:56] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): I'm adding it to my list of units I really want to see in game (in case you're being sarcastic and won't champion the unit). Thanks for that one.
[2018-07-12 18:58] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Adding anyways, it's a very good one.
[2018-07-12 19:05] supergreyman (Greyman): **Snowbird** 6 :Gold: :Red: (10 supply)
1 :Health: 
Start of turn: Gain :Attack: 
Click: Pay :Red: :Attack: to :Chill: 3
[2018-07-12 19:07] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Has Chill, I'm a fan already.
[2018-07-12 19:08] jeacaveo (jeacaveo (#NeverBrowserer)): Seems properly costed. Has my vote.
[2018-07-12 19:09] supergreyman (Greyman): I've been making simple design today...
[2018-07-12 19:09] supergreyman (Greyman): How to make this more complicated. 🤔

:Blocker: 

:Click:, Pay 2 :Gold:, :Exhaust: : Remove 1 :Exhaust: from a unit.
[2018-07-12 20:10] darkeisbein: does nothing in most sets
[2018-07-12 20:11] darkeisbein: so that's a big turn off from the unit
[2018-07-12 20:11] darkeisbein: and even then it does very little with a lot of units
[2018-07-12 20:12] darkeisbein: so like really  there is only wincer that I can think of where this would be interesting
[2018-07-12 20:15] rezyk: forcibly desyncing enemy iso, i guess .. agree that it seems very niche. also constrains design a lot
[2018-07-12 20:15] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, I was making a mini-Vai unit.
[2018-07-12 20:16] supergreyman (Greyman): As for the TT>11, it's similar to how you would deal with Electrovores.
[2018-07-12 20:18] supergreyman (Greyman): So even if you get to freeze 2 Walls, you'll only be dealing 2 real damage, which is the same as dealing 4 damage with my Wall absorbing 2, except you're wasting Red here.
[2018-07-12 20:20] supergreyman (Greyman): But I agree that it will really make freeze calculation very complicated.
[2018-07-12 20:24] darkeisbein: except I'm in a low econ scenario so I don't really mind not using a red
[2018-07-12 20:24] darkeisbein: like yeah if it is to good a rush unit in theory you just go elyot animus to counter it
[2018-07-12 20:24] darkeisbein: maybe limit the supply to 4?
[2018-07-12 20:25] darkeisbein: just so the threat indicator doesn't become totally useless
[2018-07-12 20:28] darkeisbein: also I guess vs. rhinos the click isn't even doing any damage

[2018-07-13 23:09] supergreyman (Greyman): It's pretty complicated. SDR is saying is that if the attributes of unit A are better than unit B, unit A should always be better in every situation. For example, you should always be happy to have 5HP Gauss Cannon than 3HP Gauss Cannon in every single situation.

The case for Steelsplitter is similar. If a 3HP non-blocking unit exists that has "Start of turn: Gain :Attack:". Which one should you prefer? This unit or Steelsplitter? You should always pick Steelsplitter because it has a flexibility of being an attacker or a blocker. It has options to do two different things, so having this type of flexibility is an attribute.

With Dry Eraser, it punishes units with this type of attribute. If you're one attack away from winning and you bought a Steelsplitter. Well, that would be too bad. You should've picked the one that is strictly worse than a Steelsplitter!
[2018-07-13 23:10] supergreyman (Greyman): Anyway, I wouldn't worry too much about it.

messages.author: supergreyman
messages.content:
It's pretty complicated. SDR is saying is that if the attributes of unit A are better than unit B, unit A should always be better in every situation. For example, you should always be happy to have 5HP Gauss Cannon than 3HP Gauss Cannon in every single situation.

The case for Steelsplitter is similar. If a 3HP non-blocking unit exists that has "Start of turn: Gain :Attack:". Which one should you prefer? This unit or Steelsplitter? You should always pick Steelsplitter because it has a flexibility of being an attacker or a blocker. It has options to do two different things, so having this type of flexibility is an attribute.

With Dry Eraser, it punishes units with this type of attribute. If you're one attack away from winning and you bought a Steelsplitter. Well, that would be too bad. You should've picked the one that is strictly worse than a Steelsplitter!

messages.author: supergreyman
messages.content: Anyway, I wouldn't worry too much about it.

[2018-07-18 03:24] kaelii: @Rezyk For most things its not relevant but for buying Drones, clicking Electrovores, Cauterizers and Fission turrets SoT gain :Gold: per :Engineer: is significantly better. Also something something something wasted resources ultimate edge cases that will never actually happen because it is SO good to just buy more engineers with spare gold/energy if you have Coveter going.
[2018-07-18 03:25] kaelii: Mostly I'm concerned about the drone thing. I don't want people buying it to just buy 6 drones a turn at some midgame point.
[2018-07-18 03:27] kaelii: Like buying engineers alongside Coveter is already really strong because they're basically doomed drones that have stamina instead of lifespan.
[2018-07-18 03:32] kaelii: For non-deterministic spending I'd argue that either "Doesn't spend energy unless it has to" up to the moment you go to confirm turn where it can correctly spend as much unspent energy as it can and refund gold.
[2018-07-18 03:34] kaelii: Or you could try to program it so that it spends energy first but you're allowed to click things that spend energy up to however much you generated and it will reassign spending gold for any purchases that it can.
[2018-07-18 03:35] kaelii: Personally I like the first solution a bit more, although it might be a bit more difficult to make sure players know what is being done.
[2018-07-18 03:36] kaelii: Second solution is more like clicking your attackers, clicking enemy frontline unit, clicking your four thermite cores, then unclicking some stuff to go back to 0 attack.
[2018-07-18 03:43] kaelii: Could instead do something super silly like "Start of turn: Construct a :Lifespan: 1 Galvani Drone for each Engineer" which is probably worse because the gold is delayed, and would probably be super gross on the UI
[2018-07-18 03:50] supergreyman (Greyman): Yeah, that just looks really weird.
[2018-07-18 03:50] supergreyman (Greyman): I prefer the mighty simple "Gain :Gold: for each Engineer you have".
[2018-07-18 03:52] supergreyman (Greyman): @Redrame Thanks for the video on your unit design reddit post! It's nice to see your thought process on several submissions. 👍
[2018-07-18 03:52] kaelii: Yeah there's a good bit of merit to how simple gain gold per engineer is.
